\boxed{Stoicism}

The question refers to the **Stoic epistles**, such as those attributed to Epictetus, Seneca, and Marcus Aurelius, which were widely circulated and influential during the Roman Empire. These works were written in the form of letters to convey philosophical teachings on virtue, resilience, and rational living. The popularity of these epistles reflects the enduring appeal of Stoic thought in ancient Roman society.
